meta.title: ChooseTools — Free file tools that never upload your files
meta.description: Merge, compress and convert PDF and images directly in your browser. No uploads, no limits, no waiting. Your files never leave your device.

home.heroSub: Everything runs inside your browser. Your files never leave your device — so there are no size caps, no daily limits and no waiting for uploads.
home.why1d: Other tool sites upload every file to their servers, then promise to delete it. We never receive it in the first place — processing happens on your own device.
home.why2d: No 15 MB caps, no “2 tasks per day”, no watermarks. Because nothing runs on our servers, unlimited use costs us nothing — so it costs you nothing.
home.why3d: No upload time, no queue, no download wait. A 100 MB file starts processing the moment you drop it.

privacy.note: This tool runs entirely in your browser. Your file is never sent anywhere.

pdf.name: PDF tools
pdf.desc: Merge, split, rotate, compress and convert PDF files — privately, in your browser.

image.name: Image tools
image.desc: Compress, resize and convert images without uploading them anywhere.

text.name: Text & developer tools
text.desc: Counters, converters, generators and formatters that work offline.

arabic.name: Arabic tools
arabic.desc: Tools built specifically for Arabic text and dates — not translations of English tools.

tool.dropTitle: Drop files here or click to choose
tool.dropSingle: Drop a file here or click to choose
tool.error: That file could not be processed. Check the format and try again.

merge-pdf.name: Merge PDF
merge-pdf.desc: Combine several PDF files into one, in the order you choose.
merge-pdf.long: Drop two or more PDF files, drag them into the order you want, and download a single merged document. The merge happens on your device, so even confidential contracts never leave your computer.
merge-pdf.faq1q: Is there a limit on file size or number of files?
merge-pdf.faq1a: No. Because merging runs in your browser, the only limit is your device's memory — files of hundreds of megabytes work fine on a normal laptop.
merge-pdf.faq2q: Are my PDFs uploaded to a server?
merge-pdf.faq2a: Never. The files are read and combined locally by your browser. You can disconnect from the internet after the page loads and the tool keeps working.

split-pdf.name: Split PDF
split-pdf.desc: Extract a page range from a PDF into a new file.
split-pdf.long: Choose the pages you need — for example pages 3 to 7 — and get a new PDF containing only that range. Nothing is uploaded.
split-pdf.faq1q: Can I extract a single page?
split-pdf.faq1a: Yes — set the same number as both the start and end page.
split-pdf.faq2a: No. Pages are copied exactly as they are, with no re-compression.

rotate-pdf.name: Rotate PDF
rotate-pdf.desc: Rotate all pages of a PDF by 90°, 180° or 270°.
rotate-pdf.long: Fix scanned documents that came out sideways or upside down. Rotation is lossless and instant.
rotate-pdf.faq1a: Yes — the downloaded file is saved with the new orientation and opens correctly in any PDF reader.
rotate-pdf.faq2a: No. Only the page orientation flag changes; the content is untouched.

delete-pdf-pages.name: Delete PDF pages
delete-pdf-pages.desc: Remove specific pages from a PDF file.
delete-pdf-pages.long: Type the page numbers you want to remove (e.g. 2, 5-7) and download the cleaned-up PDF.
delete-pdf-pages.faq1q: How do I write the page numbers?
delete-pdf-pages.faq1a: Use commas for single pages and a dash for ranges: “2, 5-7, 10” removes page 2, pages 5 through 7, and page 10.
delete-pdf-pages.faq2a: Your original file is never modified — it stays untouched on your device, so you can simply run the tool again.

jpg-to-pdf.name: JPG to PDF
jpg-to-pdf.desc: Turn one or more images into a single PDF document.
jpg-to-pdf.long: Drop JPG or PNG images, arrange the order, and download a PDF with one image per page. Ideal for scanned receipts and documents.
jpg-to-pdf.faq1q: Can I combine JPG and PNG in one PDF?
jpg-to-pdf.faq1a: Yes, both formats can be mixed freely in the same document.
jpg-to-pdf.faq2a: No — images are embedded at their original quality.

pdf-to-jpg.name: PDF to JPG
pdf-to-jpg.desc: Convert each PDF page into a high-quality JPG image.
pdf-to-jpg.long: Every page is rendered on your device and downloaded as an image — one JPG per page, or all pages in a ZIP.
pdf-to-jpg.faq1a: Pages are rendered at 2× scale, which is sharp enough for screens and most printing.
pdf-to-jpg.faq2q: My PDF has many pages. Is that a problem?
pdf-to-jpg.faq2a: No — pages are converted one by one, so even long documents work. You can download everything as a single ZIP.

compress-pdf.name: Compress PDF
compress-pdf.desc: Reduce PDF file size with lossless optimization.
compress-pdf.long: The file is rewritten with optimized internal structure and object streams. Text and images are not degraded — this is lossless optimization, not destructive compression.
compress-pdf.faq1q: How much smaller will my file get?
compress-pdf.faq1a: It depends on how the PDF was created. Poorly generated files can shrink a lot; already-optimized files less. The exact saving is shown before you download.
compress-pdf.faq2a: No. This optimization is fully lossless — every page looks exactly the same.

compress-image.name: Compress image
compress-image.desc: Shrink JPG, PNG and WebP file sizes right in your browser.
compress-image.long: Smart compression reduces file size dramatically while keeping images looking sharp. Perfect for websites, email attachments and forms with upload limits.
compress-image.faq1q: How much can I expect to save?
compress-image.faq1a: Typically 60–90% for photos, depending on the quality level you pick. The result and savings are shown before downloading.
compress-image.faq2q: Is there a file size limit?
compress-image.faq2a: No fixed limit — compression runs on your device, so even very large photos work.

resize-image.name: Resize image
resize-image.desc: Change image dimensions to exact pixel sizes.
resize-image.long: Enter a new width or height — the aspect ratio is kept automatically unless you turn it off. Runs instantly on your device.
resize-image.faq1a: Downscaling keeps images sharp. Upscaling beyond the original size can look soft — that's a limit of the source image, not the tool.
resize-image.faq2a: JPG, PNG and WebP. The output keeps the same format as the input.

convert-image.name: Convert image
convert-image.desc: Convert between JPG, PNG and WebP formats.
convert-image.long: Pick the output format and convert instantly. WebP output typically makes files 25–35% smaller than JPG at the same quality.
convert-image.faq1a: For websites — it is smaller than JPG and PNG at similar quality and supported by all modern browsers.
convert-image.faq2q: Does converting PNG to JPG lose transparency?
convert-image.faq2a: Yes — JPG has no transparency, so transparent areas become white. Use PNG or WebP to keep transparency.

heic-to-jpg.name: HEIC to JPG
heic-to-jpg.desc: Convert iPhone HEIC photos to universally supported JPG.
heic-to-jpg.long: iPhones save photos as HEIC, which many apps and websites reject. Convert them to JPG on your own device — your personal photos are never uploaded anywhere.
heic-to-jpg.faq1q: Why won't my HEIC photos open on Windows?
heic-to-jpg.faq1a: Windows needs a paid codec to open HEIC. Converting to JPG makes photos open everywhere without extra software.
heic-to-jpg.faq2a: Conversion uses 92% JPG quality, which is visually identical to the original for photos.

rotate-image.name: Rotate & flip image
rotate-image.desc: Rotate images by 90° steps or mirror them horizontally/vertically.
rotate-image.long: Fix photos taken sideways or create mirrored versions. All edits happen locally and download instantly.
rotate-image.faq1a: PNG output is lossless. JPG output is re-encoded at high quality (95%), which is visually indistinguishable.
rotate-image.faq2q: Can I rotate by a custom angle?
rotate-image.faq2a: This tool covers 90° steps and mirroring, which fixes orientation issues. Free-angle rotation is on our roadmap.

remove-exif.name: Remove EXIF data
remove-exif.desc: Strip GPS location and camera metadata from photos before sharing.
remove-exif.long: Photos secretly carry metadata: GPS coordinates of where they were taken, device model, date and time. This tool produces a clean copy with all of it removed — and because it runs locally, the photo you're cleaning is never itself exposed.
remove-exif.faq1a: All EXIF metadata: GPS location, camera and phone model, timestamps, software tags and thumbnail previews. Only the visible image remains.
remove-exif.faq2q: How can I verify it worked?
remove-exif.faq2a: Check the file's properties before and after (right-click → Properties → Details on Windows). The cleaned copy shows no location or camera fields.

word-counter.name: Word & character counter
word-counter.desc: Count words, characters, sentences and estimated reading time.
word-counter.long: Paste or type text and see live counts — including character counts with and without spaces, and reading time at average speed.
word-counter.faq1q: Does it work for Arabic text?
word-counter.faq1a: Yes — word boundaries are detected correctly for Arabic, English, Spanish and mixed text.
word-counter.faq2a: No. Counting happens as you type, entirely in your browser.

case-converter.name: Case converter
case-converter.desc: Convert text to UPPERCASE, lowercase, Title Case or Sentence case.
case-converter.long: Paste text, click a style, copy the result. Useful for fixing text typed with Caps Lock on.
case-converter.faq1a: The First Letter Of Each Word Is Capitalized — commonly used for headlines.
case-converter.faq2a: Arabic has no letter case, so Arabic characters pass through unchanged — only Latin letters are converted.

password-generator.name: Password generator
password-generator.desc: Generate strong random passwords with custom rules.
password-generator.long: Passwords are generated with your browser's cryptographic random generator and never leave your device — nothing is ever transmitted or stored.
password-generator.faq1q: How long should my password be?
password-generator.faq1a: At least 16 characters for important accounts. Length matters more than complexity — every extra character multiplies cracking time.
password-generator.faq2q: Is it safe to generate passwords online?
password-generator.faq2a: On this page, yes: generation uses crypto.getRandomValues() locally in your browser. You can load the page, disconnect from the internet, and generate offline.

qr-generator.name: QR code generator
qr-generator.desc: Create QR codes for links, text or Wi-Fi — free and without watermarks.
qr-generator.long: Type any text or URL and download a high-resolution PNG QR code. No sign-up, no expiring codes, no watermark.
qr-generator.faq1a: Never. The code is generated directly from your text — there is no shortening service or server that could shut down.
qr-generator.faq2a: 1024×1024 pixels PNG — sharp enough for print, business cards and posters.

json-formatter.name: JSON formatter
json-formatter.desc: Format, validate and minify JSON data.
json-formatter.long: Paste JSON to pretty-print it with proper indentation, or minify it for production. Errors are pinpointed with a clear message.
json-formatter.faq1q: Is it safe to paste API responses with sensitive data?
json-formatter.faq1a: Yes — formatting happens locally in your browser. Nothing you paste is transmitted anywhere.
json-formatter.faq2a: The text breaks JSON syntax rules — commonly a trailing comma, single quotes instead of double, or an unquoted key. The error message shows where.

base64.name: Base64 encode / decode
base64.desc: Convert text to Base64 and back, with full Unicode support.
base64.long: Encode any text — including Arabic and emoji — to Base64, or decode Base64 back to readable text.
base64.faq1a: It represents binary or special-character data using only safe ASCII characters — common in APIs, email attachments and data URLs.
base64.faq2a: No. Base64 is encoding, not encryption — anyone can decode it. Never use it to protect secrets.

remove-tashkeel.name: Remove tashkeel
remove-tashkeel.desc: Strip Arabic diacritics (harakat) from text in one click.
remove-tashkeel.long: Removes fatha, damma, kasra, sukun, shadda, tanween and tatweel from Arabic text — useful for search indexing, databases and clean typography.
remove-tashkeel.faq1q: Does it change the letters themselves?
remove-tashkeel.faq1a: No — only diacritical marks are removed. The letters, spaces and punctuation stay exactly as they are.
remove-tashkeel.faq2a: Yes — toggle “keep shadda” to remove all marks except shadda, which often carries meaning.

arabic-numerals.name: Arabic ↔ Western numerals
arabic-numerals.desc: Convert between Eastern Arabic (٠١٢٣) and Western (0123) digits.
arabic-numerals.long: Converts every digit in your text in either direction while leaving all other characters untouched. Handles Persian digit variants too.
arabic-numerals.faq1a: Confusingly, both! ٠١٢٣٤ are Eastern Arabic (Arabic-Indic) digits used in most Arabic countries; 01234 are Western Arabic digits. This tool converts between them.
arabic-numerals.faq2q: Will it change dates and phone numbers correctly?
arabic-numerals.faq2a: Yes — digits are converted one-to-one wherever they appear, so formats like dates and phone numbers keep their structure.

hijri-gregorian.name: Hijri ↔ Gregorian converter
hijri-gregorian.desc: Convert dates between the Islamic (Umm al-Qura) and Gregorian calendars.
hijri-gregorian.long: Accurate two-way conversion using the Umm al-Qura calendar — the official calendar of Saudi Arabia — with the weekday shown for every result.
hijri-gregorian.faq1a: Umm al-Qura, the calculated calendar officially used in Saudi Arabia. Actual moon sighting can differ by ±1 day for religious observances.
hijri-gregorian.faq2a: Any date from the Hijri epoch (622 CE) far into the future — enough for birthdays, contracts and historical dates.

reorder-pdf.name: Reorder PDF pages
reorder-pdf.desc: Rearrange the pages of a PDF with visual thumbnails.
reorder-pdf.long: Every page is rendered as a thumbnail on your device. Move pages into the order you want and download the rearranged file — nothing is uploaded.
reorder-pdf.faq1q: Can I move a page from the end to the beginning?
reorder-pdf.faq1a: Yes — use the arrows under each thumbnail to move pages step by step into any position.
reorder-pdf.faq2a: No. Pages are copied exactly as they are; only their order changes.

watermark-pdf.name: Add watermark to PDF
watermark-pdf.desc: Stamp a diagonal text watermark across every page.
watermark-pdf.long: Type your watermark text (e.g. CONFIDENTIAL or DRAFT), pick the opacity, and every page gets a clean diagonal stamp — processed locally.
watermark-pdf.faq1q: Can I use Arabic text in the watermark?
watermark-pdf.faq1a: Not yet — the built-in PDF fonts cover Latin letters and numbers. Arabic watermark support with embedded fonts is on our roadmap.
watermark-pdf.faq2q: Can the watermark be removed later?
watermark-pdf.faq2a: The watermark becomes part of the page content. Keep your original file — it is never modified.

page-numbers-pdf.name: Add page numbers to PDF
page-numbers-pdf.desc: Insert page numbers at the bottom of every page.
page-numbers-pdf.long: Choose a simple style (1, 2, 3) or the “1 / 12” format, and download a numbered copy of your document.
page-numbers-pdf.faq1a: Centered at the bottom of each page, in a size that stays readable without clashing with your content.
page-numbers-pdf.faq2q: Can I start numbering from a page other than 1?
page-numbers-pdf.faq2a: The current version numbers all pages from 1. Custom start pages are planned.

crop-image.name: Crop image
crop-image.desc: Crop images to standard aspect ratios like 1:1 or 16:9.
crop-image.long: Pick a target ratio and the image is center-cropped to the largest possible area — perfect for profile pictures, covers and thumbnails.
crop-image.faq1a: Square 1:1, classic 4:3 and 3:4, and widescreen 16:9 and 9:16 for stories and covers.
crop-image.faq2a: Yes — the tool keeps the largest centered region that fits your chosen ratio.

circle-crop.name: Circle crop image
circle-crop.desc: Cut a perfect circle from any photo, with transparency.
circle-crop.long: The largest centered circle is cut from your image and saved as a transparent PNG — ready for avatars and logos.
circle-crop.faq1q: Why is the output a PNG?
circle-crop.faq1a: Only PNG (and WebP) support transparency. The corners outside the circle are fully transparent.
circle-crop.faq2q: Can I choose where the circle is placed?
circle-crop.faq2a: The circle is centered automatically. For custom placement, crop the image first with the Crop tool.

blur-image.name: Blur image
blur-image.desc: Apply an adjustable gaussian blur to a photo.
blur-image.long: Choose the blur strength with a slider — useful for backgrounds, redacting details or creating soft cover images.
blur-image.faq1q: Can I blur only part of the image?
blur-image.faq1a: This tool blurs the whole image. Region-based blur is on our roadmap.
blur-image.faq2a: Strong blur (20px+) makes text unreadable, but for sensitive data we recommend cropping it out entirely instead.

image-filters.name: Image filters
image-filters.desc: Apply grayscale, sepia, invert, brightness or contrast.
image-filters.long: One-click classic filters, processed instantly on your device at full resolution.
image-filters.faq1a: No — the image keeps its exact original dimensions.
image-filters.faq2a: Apply one filter, download, then run the result through the tool again to combine effects.

image-to-base64.name: Image to Base64
image-to-base64.desc: Convert an image into a Base64 data URL for embedding.
image-to-base64.long: Get a copy-ready data URL for use in HTML, CSS or JSON — generated locally, so even private images are safe.
image-to-base64.faq1q: When should I embed images as Base64?
image-to-base64.faq1a: For small images (icons, tiny logos) where saving an HTTP request matters. Large images are better served as normal files.
image-to-base64.faq2q: Why is the Base64 string larger than my file?
image-to-base64.faq2a: Base64 encoding adds roughly 33% overhead — that is normal and expected.

favicon-generator.name: Favicon generator
favicon-generator.desc: Generate a complete favicon set from one image.
favicon-generator.long: Upload a square logo and download a ZIP with all standard sizes (16–512px), the Apple touch icon, and a ready-to-paste HTML snippet.
favicon-generator.faq1q: What image should I start from?
favicon-generator.faq1a: A square PNG or SVG of at least 512×512 pixels gives the sharpest results at every size.
favicon-generator.faq2a: 16, 32, 48, 192 and 512 pixels plus the 180px apple-touch-icon — everything modern browsers and phones request.

color-picker.name: Color picker from image
color-picker.desc: Click anywhere on an image to get exact HEX and RGB values.
color-picker.long: Upload a photo or screenshot and click any pixel — the tool builds a palette of your picked colors with one-click copy.
color-picker.faq1q: How accurate is the picked color?
color-picker.faq1a: Exact — the value is read directly from the image pixel you click.
color-picker.faq2a: Yes, every click adds to your palette (up to the last 12 picks).

svg-to-png.name: SVG to PNG
svg-to-png.desc: Convert vector SVG files to PNG at 1×–8× scale.
svg-to-png.long: Renders your SVG on your device and exports a crisp PNG — choose a higher scale for print or retina displays.
svg-to-png.faq1q: What does the scale setting do?
svg-to-png.faq1a: It multiplies the SVG's natural size: an icon of 100×100 exported at 4× becomes a 400×400 PNG.
svg-to-png.faq2a: Yes — transparent areas of the SVG stay transparent in the PNG.

id-photo.name: Passport & ID photo
id-photo.desc: Resize photos to official passport sizes: Saudi, UAE, Egypt, EU, US.
id-photo.long: Pick your country preset and get a correctly sized photo at print quality (300 DPI). The crop is biased toward the top of the frame where the face usually sits — and because it runs locally, your personal photo never leaves your device.
id-photo.faq1a: EU/Schengen 35×45mm, Saudi Arabia and Egypt 40×60mm, UAE 43×55mm, and US 51×51mm — all exported at 300 DPI.
id-photo.faq2q: Does the tool verify photo requirements?
id-photo.faq2a: It produces the correct dimensions and resolution. Requirements like background color and facial expression remain your responsibility — check your authority's guidelines.

url-encode.name: URL encode / decode
url-encode.desc: Encode text for safe use in URLs, or decode it back.
url-encode.long: Percent-encoding made instant — full Unicode support including Arabic characters in URLs.
url-encode.faq1q: When do I need URL encoding?
url-encode.faq1a: Whenever text goes into a URL: query parameters, search terms, or any characters like spaces, ?, & and non-Latin letters.
url-encode.faq2q: What is the difference with Base64?
url-encode.faq2a: URL encoding keeps text readable and is specifically for URLs; Base64 is a general binary-to-text encoding.

uuid-generator.name: UUID generator
uuid-generator.desc: Generate random version-4 UUIDs in bulk.
uuid-generator.long: Uses your browser's native crypto.randomUUID() — cryptographically strong identifiers, generated offline.
uuid-generator.faq1q: What is a UUID used for?
uuid-generator.faq1a: As a practically collision-free identifier for database records, API objects, filenames and distributed systems.
uuid-generator.faq2a: The probability is so small it is negligible in practice — you would need to generate billions per second for centuries.

hash-generator.name: Hash generator
hash-generator.desc: Compute SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384 and SHA-512 hashes of text.
hash-generator.long: Hashes update live as you type, computed with the browser's native Web Crypto API — your text never leaves the page.
hash-generator.faq1a: MD5 is cryptographically broken and browsers do not implement it natively. SHA-256 is the modern standard.
hash-generator.faq2a: No — a hash is a one-way fingerprint. The same input always gives the same hash, but the input cannot be recovered from it.

remove-duplicate-lines.name: Remove duplicate lines
remove-duplicate-lines.desc: Delete repeated lines from a list while keeping the original order.
remove-duplicate-lines.long: Paste any list — emails, keywords, URLs — and get each unique line exactly once, with a count of what was removed.
remove-duplicate-lines.faq1a: Yes — “Apple” and “apple” are treated as different lines. Use the Case Converter first to normalize if needed.
remove-duplicate-lines.faq2a: Yes, the first occurrence of each line stays in place; later repeats are removed.

text-diff.name: Text diff
text-diff.desc: Compare two texts and highlight added and removed lines.
text-diff.long: Paste two versions side by side and see exactly which lines changed — additions in green, deletions in red.
text-diff.faq1a: Line by line, using the same longest-common-subsequence approach as programming diff tools.
text-diff.faq2a: Yes — the tool is content-agnostic and works for code, contracts, articles or any text.

lorem-ipsum.name: Lorem ipsum generator
lorem-ipsum.desc: Generate placeholder paragraphs for designs and mockups.
lorem-ipsum.long: Classic lorem ipsum filler text, 1–10 paragraphs, generated instantly with one-click copy.
lorem-ipsum.faq1q: Why use lorem ipsum instead of real text?
lorem-ipsum.faq1a: Its natural letter distribution lets viewers judge a layout without being distracted by readable content.
lorem-ipsum.faq2a: Yes — sentence lengths and word order vary on every generation, like natural writing.

markdown-to-html.name: Markdown to HTML
markdown-to-html.desc: Convert Markdown syntax to clean HTML markup.
markdown-to-html.long: Powered by the modern marked parser, running entirely in your browser — paste Markdown, copy production-ready HTML.
markdown-to-html.faq1a: The full CommonMark standard: headings, emphasis, lists, links, images, code blocks, blockquotes and tables (GFM).
markdown-to-html.faq2a: The output mirrors your input. If you plan to render HTML from untrusted sources, sanitize it in your application.

csv-json.name: CSV ↔ JSON converter
csv-json.desc: Convert between CSV data and JSON arrays in both directions.
csv-json.long: Powered by the industry-standard Papa Parse engine — handles quoted fields, commas inside values, and headers correctly.
csv-json.faq1q: Does CSV→JSON use the first row as keys?
csv-json.faq1a: Yes — the header row becomes the JSON property names for every object.
csv-json.faq2q: My CSV uses semicolons — will it work?
csv-json.faq2a: Yes, the parser auto-detects common delimiters including semicolons and tabs.

tafqit.name: Tafqit — numbers to Arabic words
tafqit.desc: Convert numbers into written Arabic words for invoices and cheques.
tafqit.long: Type any amount up to billions and get the grammatically correct Arabic wording, with optional currency (SAR, AED, EGP, USD, EUR) in the legal “only and nothing more” format used on invoices.
tafqit.faq1a: Arabic invoices, cheques and contracts legally require amounts written in words — tafqit converts 15,750 into its full Arabic wording instantly.
tafqit.faq2a: Yes — the tool applies the correct forms for duals, 3–10 plurals, and the accusative used after 11+ (e.g. ألفاً / مليوناً).

extract-pdf-pages.name: Extract PDF pages
extract-pdf-pages.desc: Pull specific pages or ranges out of a PDF into a new file.
extract-pdf-pages.long: Type page ranges like “1-3, 5, 8-10” and get a new PDF containing exactly those pages, in that order — processed on your device.
extract-pdf-pages.faq1a: Single pages (5), ranges (2-7), or any comma-separated mix like 1-3, 6, 9-12.
extract-pdf-pages.faq2a: Never — a brand-new PDF is created and your original stays untouched on your device.

pdf-to-text.name: PDF to text
pdf-to-text.desc: Extract all text content from a PDF into plain text.
pdf-to-text.long: Reads the text layer of every page and gives you clean, copyable text — with a one-click .txt download. Works with Arabic, Latin and Spanish documents.
pdf-to-text.faq1q: Why did my scanned PDF return no text?
pdf-to-text.faq1a: Scanned PDFs are images without a text layer. Text extraction needs a digitally created PDF; OCR for scans is on our roadmap.
pdf-to-text.faq2a: Text is extracted page by page in reading order, but complex layouts (columns, tables) become plain flowing text.

png-to-pdf.name: PNG to PDF
png-to-pdf.desc: Combine one or more PNG images into a single PDF.
png-to-pdf.long: Each image becomes a page at its original resolution — transparency is preserved on the PDF page. Perfect for screenshots and scans.
png-to-pdf.faq1q: Can I control the page order?
png-to-pdf.faq1a: Pages follow the order you add files in — remove and re-add to rearrange, or use the Reorder PDF tool afterwards.
png-to-pdf.faq2a: No — PNGs are embedded losslessly at their full original resolution.

pdf-metadata.name: Edit PDF metadata
pdf-metadata.desc: View and edit a PDF's title, author, subject and keywords.
pdf-metadata.long: Metadata determines how a document appears in search results and file managers. Edit the fields and download an updated copy — the content itself is untouched.
pdf-metadata.faq1a: Search engines and document systems index title and author fields; correct metadata makes your PDFs findable and professional.
pdf-metadata.faq2q: Does editing metadata change the document content?
pdf-metadata.faq2a: No — only the invisible information fields change. Every page stays byte-identical.

grayscale-pdf.name: Grayscale PDF
grayscale-pdf.desc: Convert a color PDF to black & white.
grayscale-pdf.long: Every page is re-rendered in grayscale on your device — ideal for print-friendly versions and reducing toner use.
grayscale-pdf.faq1a: No — pages are converted to high-resolution grayscale images, so text selection is lost. Keep your color original for editing.
grayscale-pdf.faq2q: Why did my file get bigger or smaller?
grayscale-pdf.faq2a: Pages become compressed images; text-heavy files may grow slightly while photo-heavy files often shrink.

flip-image.name: Flip image
flip-image.desc: Mirror an image horizontally or vertically.
flip-image.long: One click flips your photo along either axis at full quality — useful for selfies, scans and design assets.
flip-image.faq1q: What is the difference between flip and rotate?
flip-image.faq1a: Flipping mirrors the image (left becomes right); rotating turns it around its center. We have a separate Rotate tool.
flip-image.faq2a: No — pixels are rearranged, not recompressed, when the source is PNG; JPGs are re-encoded at high quality.

png-to-jpg.name: PNG to JPG
png-to-jpg.desc: Convert PNG images to JPG with white background fill.
png-to-jpg.long: Transparent areas are flattened onto white and the image is saved as a high-quality JPG — usually far smaller than the PNG.
png-to-jpg.faq1a: JPG does not support transparency, so transparent areas are filled with white.
png-to-jpg.faq2q: How much smaller will the file be?
png-to-jpg.faq2a: Photos typically shrink 60–80%. Graphics with few colors may not shrink much — those are better kept as PNG.

webp-to-jpg.name: WebP to JPG
webp-to-jpg.desc: Convert WebP images to universally compatible JPG.
webp-to-jpg.long: WebP files sometimes fail to open in older software — convert them to JPG in one click, right in your browser.
webp-to-jpg.faq1a: Although WebP is efficient, some editors, printers and older systems still reject it. JPG opens everywhere.
webp-to-jpg.faq2a: The conversion re-encodes at 92% quality — visually identical for photos in practice.

jpg-to-webp.name: JPG to WebP
jpg-to-webp.desc: Convert JPG/PNG images to modern, smaller WebP.
jpg-to-webp.long: WebP files are typically 25–35% smaller than JPG at the same visual quality — ideal for faster websites.
jpg-to-webp.faq1a: All modern browsers (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge) support WebP natively — over 97% of web users.
jpg-to-webp.faq2q: When should I not use WebP?
jpg-to-webp.faq2a: For print workflows or software that requires JPG/PNG input. For the web, WebP is almost always the better choice.

add-text-to-image.name: Add text to image
add-text-to-image.desc: Write text onto a photo — Arabic fully supported.
add-text-to-image.long: Choose position, color and size; the text is drawn with a clean outline so it stays readable on any background. Arabic script shapes correctly.
add-text-to-image.faq1a: Yes — the browser's native text engine handles Arabic letter shaping and right-to-left direction correctly.
add-text-to-image.faq2a: The current version adds a single line per pass — run the result through the tool again for additional lines.

pixelate-image.name: Pixelate image
pixelate-image.desc: Apply a retro pixel-block effect or censor details.
pixelate-image.long: Choose the pixel size with a slider — from a subtle mosaic to heavy censoring blocks, processed instantly on your device.
pixelate-image.faq1q: Is pixelation safe for hiding sensitive data?
pixelate-image.faq1a: Heavy pixelation (20+) is hard to reverse, but for truly sensitive information cropping it out entirely is safer.
pixelate-image.faq2q: What is the difference with blur?
pixelate-image.faq2a: Blur softens smoothly; pixelation creates hard square blocks — the classic censoring and retro-game look.

round-corners.name: Round image corners
round-corners.desc: Add rounded corners to any image, with transparency.
round-corners.long: Pick the corner radius with a slider and download a PNG with smooth transparent corners — perfect for app icons and UI assets.
round-corners.faq1a: Rounded corners need transparency, which JPG cannot store. PNG keeps the corners crisp on any background.
round-corners.faq2q: Can I make a full circle?
round-corners.faq2a: At 50% radius on a square image you get a perfect circle — or use our dedicated Circle Crop tool.

reverse-text.name: Reverse text
reverse-text.desc: Reverse characters, word order, or line order.
reverse-text.long: Three one-click modes: flip every character, reverse the word sequence, or invert line order — handy for puzzles, data fixes and fun.
reverse-text.faq1a: Yes — reversal operates on Unicode characters, though connected Arabic script may look unusual reversed, as expected.
reverse-text.faq2q: What is line reversal useful for?
reverse-text.faq2a: Flipping chronological lists, log files, or rankings without a spreadsheet.

text-to-slug.name: Text to URL slug
text-to-slug.desc: Turn any title into a clean, URL-safe slug.
text-to-slug.long: Lowercases, strips accents, and replaces spaces and symbols with hyphens as you type. Arabic letters are kept — modern browsers and search engines fully support Arabic URLs.
text-to-slug.faq1q: Are Arabic slugs good for SEO?
text-to-slug.faq1a: Yes — Google indexes and displays Arabic URLs correctly, and readable Arabic slugs improve click-through for Arabic audiences.
text-to-slug.faq2a: Everything except letters and digits becomes a hyphen; accented Latin letters are converted to their plain form (é → e).

binary-text.name: Binary ↔ text
binary-text.desc: Convert text to binary code and back.
binary-text.long: Encodes text as UTF-8 bytes shown in 8-bit binary groups — and decodes them back. Works with any language including Arabic.
binary-text.faq1q: Why does one Arabic letter become two bytes?
binary-text.faq1a: UTF-8 encodes non-Latin characters using multiple bytes — that is the standard encoding of the modern web.
binary-text.faq2q: What format does the decoder expect?
binary-text.faq2a: Groups of up to 8 binary digits separated by spaces, e.g. 01001000 01101001.

morse-code.name: Morse code translator
morse-code.desc: Translate text to Morse code and back.
morse-code.long: International Morse for letters, numbers and common punctuation — words separated by “/”, letters by spaces.
morse-code.faq1a: Letters are separated by single spaces and words by a forward slash (/), the common text convention.
morse-code.faq2a: The international standard covers Latin letters and digits; Arabic Morse variants exist but are not standardized, so they are not included.

number-base.name: Number base converter
number-base.desc: Convert between binary, octal, decimal and hexadecimal.
number-base.long: Type a number, pick its base, and read it instantly in all four bases — with one-click copy for each.
number-base.faq1q: Can I paste values with prefixes like 0x?
number-base.faq1a: Yes — 0x, 0b and 0o prefixes are stripped automatically before conversion.
number-base.faq2a: Anything within JavaScript's safe integer range — over 9 quadrillion, far beyond everyday use.

roman-numerals.name: Roman numerals converter
roman-numerals.desc: Convert numbers to Roman numerals and back.
roman-numerals.long: Type 2026 to get MMXXVI — or paste a Roman numeral to decode it. Validation rejects malformed numerals like IIII.
roman-numerals.faq1a: Standard Roman numerals cover 1 to 3999 — there is no standard zero or larger notation.
roman-numerals.faq2a: Yes — only canonical numerals are decoded; nonstandard forms like IM or VV are rejected.

random-number.name: Random number generator
random-number.desc: Generate true random numbers in any range.
random-number.long: Uses your browser's cryptographic randomness (not weak pseudo-random) with unbiased sampling — suitable for draws and giveaways.
random-number.faq1q: Is this fair for a giveaway draw?
random-number.faq1a: Yes — values come from crypto.getRandomValues with rejection sampling, so every number in the range is exactly equally likely.
random-number.faq2q: Can the same number appear twice?
random-number.faq2a: Yes, draws are independent — like rolling dice. For unique picks, generate one at a time and remove winners from your list.

color-converter.name: Color converter
color-converter.desc: Convert colors between HEX, RGB and HSL live.
color-converter.long: Pick a color or paste a HEX code and read all three formats instantly, with a live swatch and one-click copy.
color-converter.faq1q: Which format should I use in CSS?
color-converter.faq1a: All three work everywhere. HEX is compact, RGB is intuitive, and HSL is easiest for creating lighter/darker variations.
color-converter.faq2a: Yes — shorthand like #fa0 is expanded to #ffaa00 automatically.

css-minifier.name: CSS minifier
css-minifier.desc: Compress CSS by removing comments and whitespace.
css-minifier.long: Strips everything the browser doesn't need and shows exactly how many percent you saved — typically 20–40%.
css-minifier.faq1q: Is minified CSS safe to deploy?
css-minifier.faq1a: Yes — only comments and insignificant whitespace are removed; selectors and values are untouched.
css-minifier.faq2q: Should I keep the original file?
css-minifier.faq2a: Always — edit the readable version and minify as a final build step.

html-encode.name: HTML encode / decode
html-encode.desc: Escape HTML special characters or decode entities back.
html-encode.long: Converts <, >, &, quotes into safe entities for embedding code in web pages — and decodes any entity back to text.
html-encode.faq1q: When do I need HTML encoding?
html-encode.faq1a: Whenever you display user text or code samples inside HTML — unescaped < and & break the page or enable injection.
html-encode.faq2a: Yes — &, ©,   and every standard named or numeric entity are decoded.

timestamp-converter.name: Unix timestamp converter
timestamp-converter.desc: Convert Unix timestamps to readable dates and back.
timestamp-converter.long: Paste a 10- or 13-digit timestamp or any date string and read it as Unix seconds, milliseconds, ISO 8601 and your local time.
timestamp-converter.faq1q: What is the difference between 10 and 13 digits?
timestamp-converter.faq1a: 10 digits are seconds since 1970; 13 digits are milliseconds. The tool detects which one you pasted.
timestamp-converter.faq2a: ISO output is UTC (the Z suffix); the Local row uses your device's timezone.

sort-lines.name: Sort lines
sort-lines.desc: Sort text lines A→Z, Z→A, by length, or shuffle randomly.
sort-lines.long: Language-aware sorting that handles Arabic, Spanish and English alphabets correctly — plus a random shuffle for draws.
sort-lines.faq1a: Yes — sorting uses locale-aware collation, so Arabic letters follow proper Arabic alphabetical order.
sort-lines.faq2a: Empty lines are removed during sorting, leaving a clean list.

find-replace.name: Find & replace
find-replace.desc: Replace text in bulk, with optional regex support.
find-replace.long: Simple find-and-replace with match counting — switch on Regex mode for patterns, capture-free replacements and case control.
find-replace.faq1a: JavaScript regular expressions — character classes, quantifiers, anchors, alternation and more.
find-replace.faq2a: By default no — enable “Match case” for exact-case replacement.

arabic-keyboard.name: Arabic keyboard
arabic-keyboard.desc: Type Arabic with an on-screen keyboard — no Arabic layout needed.
arabic-keyboard.long: Click letters to compose Arabic text on any device or computer without Arabic keyboard support, then copy with one click. Includes hamza forms and Arabic punctuation.
arabic-keyboard.faq1a: Anyone on a device without an Arabic layout — travelers, students, and Arabic speakers using borrowed or foreign computers.
arabic-keyboard.faq2q: Can I mix typing and clicking?
arabic-keyboard.faq2a: Yes — the text area accepts normal typing too, so you can combine both.

arabic-transliteration.name: Arabic transliteration
arabic-transliteration.desc: Convert Arabic script to readable Latin letters.
arabic-transliteration.long: Transliterates Arabic text letter-by-letter into a readable romanization — useful for names, addresses and language learning.
arabic-transliteration.faq1a: No — transliteration converts the sounds/letters, not the meaning. مرحبا becomes marhba, not “hello”.
arabic-transliteration.faq2a: A simplified readable scheme (kh, sh, gh, dh for خ ش غ ذ) rather than strict academic notation — optimized for everyday readability.

zakat-calculator.name: Zakat calculator
zakat-calculator.desc: Calculate the 2.5% zakat due on your wealth.
zakat-calculator.long: Enter your cash, gold/investments and outstanding debts — the tool computes your zakat base and the amount due, entirely on your device so your finances stay private.
zakat-calculator.faq1a: Zakatable wealth (cash + gold/silver/investments − debts due) held for a lunar year is due at 2.5% if it exceeds the nisab (value of 85g of gold).
zakat-calculator.faq2a: Completely — nothing you type is transmitted anywhere. The calculation runs in your browser and vanishes when you close the page.

age-calculator.name: Age calculator
age-calculator.desc: Calculate your exact age — with the Hijri date of birth.
age-calculator.long: Enter your birth date and see your precise age in years, months and days, total days lived, your Hijri (Umm al-Qura) birth date, and the countdown to your next birthday.
age-calculator.faq1q: How accurate is the Hijri conversion?
age-calculator.faq1a: It uses the official Umm al-Qura calendar built into your browser — the same calendar used in Saudi Arabia.
age-calculator.faq2q: Does it account for leap years?
age-calculator.faq2a: Yes — the year/month/day arithmetic handles leap years and varying month lengths exactly.

excel-to-csv.name: Excel to CSV
excel-to-csv.desc: Convert Excel spreadsheets (.xlsx) to CSV files.
excel-to-csv.long: Reads the first worksheet — including formula results, dates and rich text — and produces a clean CSV with UTF-8 encoding, so Arabic and accented text open correctly in any program.
excel-to-csv.faq1a: Yes — the calculated result of each formula is exported, not the formula itself.
excel-to-csv.faq2q: Why does my CSV open with broken Arabic characters?
excel-to-csv.faq2a: Our CSV includes a UTF-8 byte-order mark specifically so Excel opens Arabic and accented text correctly.

csv-to-excel.name: CSV to Excel
csv-to-excel.desc: Convert CSV files into formatted Excel spreadsheets.
csv-to-excel.long: Handles quoted fields, commas inside values and auto-detected delimiters (semicolons, tabs) — the output has a bold header row and auto-sized columns.
csv-to-excel.faq1a: Yes, the parser auto-detects commas, semicolons and tabs.
csv-to-excel.faq2a: No fixed limit — files with tens of thousands of rows convert fine; very large files just take a few seconds.

excel-to-json.name: Excel to JSON
excel-to-json.desc: Convert Excel spreadsheets to a JSON array of objects.
excel-to-json.long: The first row becomes the property names and each following row becomes an object — copy the result or download it as a .json file.
excel-to-json.faq1a: Empty cells become null values, keeping every object's structure consistent.
excel-to-json.faq2a: Excel dates are exported in the universal YYYY-MM-DD format.

json-to-excel.name: JSON to Excel
json-to-excel.desc: Convert a JSON array into a formatted Excel file.
json-to-excel.long: Paste an array of objects — keys become column headers (merged across all objects), nested objects are stringified, and the sheet gets a bold header with auto-sized columns.
json-to-excel.faq1q: What if objects have different keys?
json-to-excel.faq1a: The union of all keys becomes the column set; missing values are left as empty cells.
json-to-excel.faq2q: Can I convert a single object?
json-to-excel.faq2a: Yes — a single object becomes a one-row spreadsheet.

mt940-to-excel.name: MT940 to Excel
mt940-to-excel.desc: Convert MT940 bank statements (SWIFT) into Excel.
mt940-to-excel.long: Banks like ING, Rabobank and ABN AMRO export statements in the MT940 format (.940/.sta). This tool parses every transaction — date, description, debit, credit, reference — into a clean Excel sheet, entirely on your device, so your banking data never leaves your computer.
mt940-to-excel.faq1a: Completely — the statement is parsed in your browser and nothing is transmitted anywhere. Disconnect from the internet and the tool still works.
mt940-to-excel.faq2a: Any bank exporting standard SWIFT MT940 — including ING, Rabobank, ABN AMRO, Deutsche Bank and most European banks. Reversal transactions (RC/RD) are handled correctly.

footer.rights: Free browser-based file tools. Your files never leave your device.

about.title: About ChooseTools
body: ChooseTools is a collection of free file tools that run entirely inside your browser. Unlike traditional tool websites, we never receive your files: merging a PDF, compressing a photo or generating a QR code all happen locally on your own device.

This architecture has three consequences we are proud of. First, privacy — a file that is never transmitted can never be leaked, logged or retained. Second, speed — there is no upload or download time, so even large files process instantly. Third, honesty — because processing costs us nothing, we can offer genuinely unlimited use with no file-size caps, daily quotas or watermarks.

The platform is fully available in English, Arabic and Spanish, with every explanation written natively rather than machine-translated. Our Arabic-exclusive tools — like tashkeel removal, Hijri date conversion and tafqit — are built specifically for Arabic users, not adapted from English tools.
ChooseTools is built and maintained by hoursmedia. New tools ship every week; if you are missing one, we would love to hear about it.

contact.title: Contact
body: Have a question, found a bug, or want to suggest a new tool? We read every message.
Email us at: contact@choosetools.com (update this address after connecting your domain mailbox).
Because all processing happens in your browser, we cannot see or recover any file you worked on — for file-specific issues, please describe the file type and the steps that led to the problem.
ChooseTools is operated by hoursmedia, Netherlands.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
body: Last updated: July 2026. This policy explains what data ChooseTools (operated by hoursmedia) does and does not process when you use this website.

Your files. All tools on this site process files locally in your browser using JavaScript and WebAssembly. Your files are never uploaded to, stored on, or seen by our servers. You can verify this by disconnecting from the internet after a tool page loads — the tool keeps working.

Analytics. We use privacy-respecting aggregate analytics to understand which tools are used and improve the site. This may include pages visited, browser type, country-level location and referral source. It never includes the content of your files or texts.

Advertising. This site may display advertisements served by Google AdSense. Google and its partners may use cookies to serve ads based on your prior visits to this or other websites. You can opt out of personalized advertising by visiting Google Ads Settings (adssettings.google.com). EU/EEA/UK visitors are shown a consent banner in accordance with the GDPR and are served non-personalized ads unless consent is given.
Cookies. Apart from the advertising and consent cookies described above, this site sets no tracking cookies. Your language preference may be stored locally in your browser.

Newsletter. If you subscribe to our newsletter, we store your email address with our email provider solely to send you the newsletter. Every email contains a one-click unsubscribe link, and we use double opt-in.
Your rights. Under the GDPR you have the right to access, correct or delete personal data we hold about you (which, for most visitors, is none). Contact us using the details on the Contact page.
Changes. We may update this policy as the site evolves; the date above always reflects the latest revision.

terms.title: Terms of Service
body: Last updated: July 2026. By using ChooseTools you agree to these terms.

The service. ChooseTools provides free, browser-based file utilities. All processing happens on your device; we never receive your files and therefore have no ability to access, restore or back them up.

Acceptable use. You may use the tools for any lawful purpose, personal or commercial. You may not attempt to disrupt the service, scrape it at abusive volume, or use it to process material you have no right to process.

No warranty. The tools are provided “as is”, without warranty of any kind. While we test every tool carefully, you are responsible for verifying results before relying on them — always keep your original files.

Limitation of liability. To the maximum extent permitted by law, hoursmedia is not liable for any damages arising from the use of this site, including data loss. Since your original files never leave your device and are never modified by us, your originals always remain in your possession.
Intellectual property. The site design, texts and code are the property of hoursmedia. The files you process remain entirely yours; we claim no rights over them.
Changes. We may modify these terms or discontinue tools at any time. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance.
